description: Introduce ArgoCD as the GitOps deployment engine for all services in The Data Platform, alongside Tilt for local development hot-reloading. ArgoCD manages the outer deployment loop (git commit → deploy) for dev/staging/production environments, while Tilt manages the inner development loop (code change → live reload) on a local k3d cluster. A dummy schema-registry service serves as the first ArgoCD-managed deployment to validate the entire pipeline end-to-end.

userStories.title: Install ArgoCD as Layer 2 platform service
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want ArgoCD installed as a Layer 2 platform service so that it can manage Layer 3 application deployments via GitOps.

Add ArgoCD Helm chart installation to monorepo/infra/platform/:
- Install argo-cd Helm chart (pin specific version) into argocd namespace using helm.v3.Release (matching cert-manager/monitoring pattern)
- Non-HA mode for local/dev
- Create ArgoCD Pulumi component in monorepo/infra/platform/charts/argocd.ts
- Use resourceTier pattern: "minimal" preset (~400 Mi total requests)
- Disable Dex, ApplicationSet controller, and Notifications controller for local to save ~200 Mi memory
- Configure repo credentials via Pulumi ESC (argocd:repoUrl, argocd:sshPrivateKey as fn::secret); skip for local (uses file:// URL)
- Configure repo-server for monorepo: shallow clones, sparse checkout (only monorepo/deploy/), server.repo.cache.expiration for disk management
- Expose ArgoCD UI via Traefik ingress (argocd.localhost locally)
- Update local-up.sh and local-down.sh scripts
- Document minimum 6 GB Docker memory allocation in prerequisites
- Document that cluster teardown destroys all ArgoCD state (expected for ephemeral local clusters)
acceptanceCriteria: ArgoCD Pulumi component exists at monorepo/infra/platform/charts/argocd.ts
ArgoCD installs into argocd namespace via helm.v3.Release
Dex, ApplicationSet controller, and Notifications controller are disabled for local
Repo credentials configured via Pulumi ESC with fn::secret
Repo-server configured for monorepo efficiency (shallow clones, sparse checkout)
ArgoCD UI exposed via Traefik ingress at argocd.localhost
local-up.sh and local-down.sh updated for ArgoCD lifecycle
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Follows existing cert-manager/monitoring Helm chart pattern in monorepo/infra/platform/.

userStories.title: Create dummy schema-registry FastAPI service
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want a minimal schema-registry FastAPI service so that I have a concrete deployment target to validate the full GitOps pipeline end-to-end.

Create monorepo/services/schema-registry/:
- Minimal FastAPI app with /health, /schemas, /schemas/{name} endpoints
- Dockerfile (multi-stage build, Python 3.12 slim base)
- requirements.txt (fastapi, uvicorn)
- Register in release-please-config.json and .release-please-manifest.json
acceptanceCriteria: FastAPI app exists at monorepo/services/schema-registry/main.py
/health endpoint returns {"status": "healthy"}
/schemas endpoint returns {"schemas": []}
/schemas/{name} endpoint returns stub response
Multi-stage Dockerfile exists with Python 3.12 slim base
requirements.txt includes fastapi and uvicorn
Service registered in release-please-config.json and .release-please-manifest.json
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Simple stub service — the goal is pipeline validation, not real functionality.

userStories.title: Create shared Helm library chart
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want a shared Helm library chart so that service charts can reuse common templates and maintain consistent conventions.

Create monorepo/deploy/charts/common/ as a Helm library chart (type: library):
- Common templates: _deployment.tpl, _service.tpl, _ingress.tpl
- Shared helpers: consistent app.kubernetes.io/* labels, naming conventions
- Standard security context defaults (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem)
- Standard health probe templates (liveness, readiness, startup)
- Service charts depend on the library via Chart.yaml dependencies
acceptanceCriteria: Library chart exists at monorepo/deploy/charts/common/ with type: library in Chart.yaml
Common templates exist: _deployment.tpl, _service.tpl, _ingress.tpl, _helpers.tpl
Templates produce consistent app.kubernetes.io/* labels
Security context defaults include runAsNonRoot and readOnlyRootFilesystem
Health probe templates cover liveness, readiness, and startup probes
helm lint passes on the library chart
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Elevated from p2 to priority 1 because TDP-005-02 (schema-registry Helm chart) depends on it.

userStories.title: Application secrets management strategy
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want a clear pattern for flowing application-level secrets into ArgoCD-managed deployments so that services can securely access credentials without storing them in Git.

Define how application-level secrets flow into ArgoCD-managed deployments:
- Store secrets in Pulumi ESC with fn::secret
- Pulumi creates K8s Secrets in application namespaces during platform bootstrap (Layer 2 concern — Pulumi owns the Secret objects, ArgoCD does not)
- Helm charts reference secrets via secretKeyRef in env vars or volume mounts
- Document the pattern for adding new application secrets
- Verify secrets are available before ArgoCD syncs application deployments
acceptanceCriteria: Pulumi code creates K8s Secrets in tdp namespace from ESC secrets
Helm chart values support secretKeyRef references for environment variables
Documentation exists for the pattern of adding new application secrets
Secrets are created during platform bootstrap (before ArgoCD syncs apps)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Builds on existing Pulumi ESC infrastructure from commit ba99cf1.

userStories.title: Create Helm chart for schema-registry stub
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want a Helm chart for the schema-registry service so that it can be deployed consistently across local, dev, and production environments.

Create monorepo/deploy/charts/schema-registry/ (depends on common library chart):
- Chart.yaml (with dependency on common library chart), values.yaml, values.schema.json, per-environment value files, .helmignore
- Templates: Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ConfigMap, Ingress, NOTES.txt
- Health probes: liveness on /health (httpGet, port 8000, initialDelaySeconds: 5, periodSeconds: 10), readiness (initialDelaySeconds: 3, periodSeconds: 5), startupProbe for slow starts
- Security context: runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
- Image tag parameterized: image.repository + image.tag
- imagePullSecrets support for private registries (ghcr.io in production)
- Helm test hooks in templates/tests/test-connection.yaml
- Decouple chart version from app version
acceptanceCriteria: Chart exists at monorepo/deploy/charts/schema-registry/ with Chart.yaml depending on common library
values.yaml, values.schema.json, values-local.yaml, values-dev.yaml, values-production.yaml exist
Templates include Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ConfigMap, Ingress, NOTES.txt
Health probes configured: liveness on /health:8000, readiness, startup
Security context sets runAsNonRoot and readOnlyRootFilesystem
Image tag parameterized via image.repository and image.tag
imagePullSecrets configurable for private registries
Helm test hook exists at templates/tests/test-connection.yaml
helm lint passes
helm template renders valid YAML for each environment values file
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Depends on US-003 (common library chart). humanReview: true — major deliverable, verify chart structure and template correctness.

userStories.title: Create ArgoCD Application manifests
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want ArgoCD Application CRDs so that ArgoCD can manage schema-registry deployments across environments.

Create monorepo/deploy/argocd/:
- appproject.yaml — TDP AppProject with explicit restrictions (sourceRepos: only platform GitHub repo, destinations: only tdp namespace, clusterResourceWhitelist: deny cluster-scoped resources)
- apps/schema-registry.yaml — Application CRD with finalizers, syncOptions (CreateNamespace=true, ServerSideApply=true), retry policy (limit: 5, exponential backoff), targetRevision: main for dev
- Per-environment sync policies: automated prune+selfHeal for dev, manual for production
- Document the promotion workflow (update image tag → commit → ArgoCD syncs)
acceptanceCriteria: AppProject manifest exists at monorepo/deploy/argocd/appproject.yaml
AppProject restricts sourceRepos to platform GitHub repo only
AppProject restricts destinations to tdp namespace
Application manifest exists at monorepo/deploy/argocd/apps/schema-registry.yaml
Application has resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io finalizer
syncOptions include CreateNamespace=true and ServerSideApply=true
Retry policy configured with limit 5 and exponential backoff
Dev uses automated sync with prune and selfHeal
Production uses manual sync without auto-prune
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on US-005 (schema-registry Helm chart must exist for Application to reference).

userStories.title: Set up Tiltfile for local development
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want a Tiltfile for local development so that I can make code changes and see them reflected in running pods within seconds.

Create monorepo/Tiltfile:
- docker_build() for schema-registry with live_update (sync Python files, restart uvicorn on dependency changes)
- helm() to render the schema-registry Helm chart with values-local.yaml
- k8s_resource() for port-forwarding, log streaming, readiness
- local_resource() for running tests, linting
- Point at local k3d cluster and registry
acceptanceCriteria: Tiltfile exists at monorepo/Tiltfile
docker_build() configured for schema-registry with live_update
helm() renders schema-registry chart with values-local.yaml
k8s_resource() configures port-forwarding and readiness
local_resource() defined for test and lint tasks
Tiltfile points at local k3d cluster and k3d-tdp-local-registry
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Depends on US-005 (Helm chart) and US-002 (FastAPI service). humanReview: true — Tiltfile is complex with many moving parts.

userStories.title: CI pipeline for image builds
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want a CI pipeline that builds and publishes container images on release so that ArgoCD can deploy versioned images to environments.

Update post-release.yml and create supporting scripts:
- Build service images on release: [published] event (not on every push)
- Tag with semver version from release; remove or restrict latest tag
- Add Docker layer caching (cache-from: type=gha, cache-to: type=gha,mode=max)
- Create monorepo/scripts/build-image.sh — called by post-release.yml for local/CI parity (accepts service name, resolves Dockerfile path)
- Use Bazel for code build/test, Docker for image build
acceptanceCriteria: post-release.yml builds container images on release published event
Images tagged with semver version from release (no latest in production)
Docker layer caching configured with GitHub Actions cache
monorepo/scripts/build-image.sh exists and is called by post-release.yml
build-image.sh accepts service name and resolves Dockerfile path
Pipeline is reproducible locally via build-image.sh
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Depends on US-002 (FastAPI service with Dockerfile). humanReview: true — CI pipeline changes need careful review.

userStories.title: Add ArgoCD bootstrapping to Tiltfile
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want to optionally boot ArgoCD in my local k3d cluster so that I can test ArgoCD Application manifests and GitOps configuration locally.

Extend Tiltfile to optionally boot ArgoCD locally:
- helm_resource() for ArgoCD Helm chart (disabled by default, enable via tilt up -- --argocd)
- local_resource() to apply ArgoCD Application manifests on file change
- When ArgoCD mode is active, Tilt manages ArgoCD but ArgoCD manages the app (Tilt's docker_build is disabled; ArgoCD pulls from registry)
- Document the two modes: "tilt-only" (default) and "tilt+argocd" (for testing GitOps config)
- Include guard that detects running ArgoCD Application resources and warns/exits before taking direct ownership
acceptanceCriteria: helm_resource() for ArgoCD Helm chart in Tiltfile, disabled by default
ArgoCD mode enabled via tilt up -- --argocd flag
local_resource() applies ArgoCD Application manifests on file change
In ArgoCD mode, docker_build is disabled and ArgoCD manages app deployments
Guard prevents concurrent Tilt and ArgoCD ownership of same resources
Two modes documented: tilt-only (default) and tilt+argocd
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Depends on US-001 (ArgoCD install) and US-007 (Tiltfile).

userStories.title: Helm chart linting and testing in CI
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want Helm chart validation in CI so that chart errors are caught before merge.

Add Helm chart validation to the CI pipeline:
- helm lint on all charts in monorepo/deploy/charts/*/
- helm template renders valid YAML for each environment's value files
- Values schema validation (values.schema.json)
- Run on PRs that touch monorepo/deploy/
- Script in monorepo/scripts/lint-charts.sh for local reproducibility
acceptanceCriteria: CI runs helm lint on all charts in monorepo/deploy/charts/
CI runs helm template for each environment values file
values.schema.json validation runs in CI
Chart validation triggers on PRs touching monorepo/deploy/
monorepo/scripts/lint-charts.sh exists for local reproducibility
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on US-005 (Helm charts must exist to lint).

userStories.title: Image scanning and CycloneDX SBOM generation
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want image vulnerability scanning and SBOM generation so that the supply chain security meets ARCHITECTURE.md standards.

Add supply chain security to the image build pipeline:
- Trivy image vulnerability scanning — fail build on CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs
- CycloneDX SBOM generation for each image
- Attach SBOM to GitHub Release as artifact
- Optionally push SBOM as OCI artifact alongside the image
- Add to post-release.yml and monorepo/scripts/build-image.sh
acceptanceCriteria: Trivy scans images in post-release.yml and fails on CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs
CycloneDX SBOM generated for each image
SBOM attached to GitHub Release as artifact
Scanning and SBOM generation integrated into monorepo/scripts/build-image.sh
Pipeline fails clearly on vulnerability threshold breach
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Depends on US-008 (CI pipeline). humanReview: true — security scanning configuration needs careful verification.

userStories.title: ArgoCD RBAC and AppProject hardening
userStories.description:
As a platform engineer, I want hardened ArgoCD access controls so that production deployments follow the principle of least privilege.

Harden ArgoCD access controls:
- AppProject sourceRepos restricted to platform GitHub repo only
- AppProject destinations restricted to tdp namespace and cluster
- clusterResourceWhitelist / namespaceResourceWhitelist to prevent deploying cluster-scoped resources
- ArgoCD ClusterRole scoped to only managed namespaces (not cluster-admin in production)
- Baseline NetworkPolicies for argocd namespace (allow Traefik ingress to UI, inter-component traffic, egress to K8s API and GitHub)
- Document that Flannel (k3d default) does not enforce NetworkPolicies locally
acceptanceCriteria: AppProject sourceRepos restricted to platform GitHub repo
AppProject destinations restricted to tdp namespace
clusterResourceWhitelist denies cluster-scoped resources by default
ArgoCD ClusterRole scoped to managed namespaces only
NetworkPolicies exist for argocd namespace
Documentation notes Flannel does not enforce NetworkPolicies locally
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Some overlap with US-006 AppProject restrictions — this story hardens beyond the initial setup.

userStories.title: Environment promotion workflow documentation
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want clear documentation of the version promotion workflow so that I can confidently deploy and roll back services across environments.

Document the version promotion workflow:
- How release-please creates tags and triggers image builds
- How to promote a version to dev (update values-dev.yaml image tag)
- How to promote to production (update values-production.yaml image tag)
- How ArgoCD detects and syncs the change
- Rollback procedure (revert the values file commit; verify previous image version still exists in registry)
- ServiceDeployment component deprecation path: document that it is no longer used for Layer 3 services once ArgoCD takes over
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation covers release-please → image build → promotion flow
Dev promotion procedure documented (values-dev.yaml update)
Production promotion procedure documented (values-production.yaml update)
Rollback procedure documented with image registry verification step
ServiceDeployment deprecation note added to monorepo/infra/components/service-deployment.ts
Documentation lives in monorepo/deploy/README.md or monorepo/infra/README.md
